content: We can adapt coordinate-wise normalization from neuroimaging spatial alignment to solve a key failure mode of 2025 ICM curiosity autonomous agents: the over-weighting of noisy state observations in unstructured high-dimensional state spaces (e.g. visual navigation). This method would normalize each spatial coordinate of an agent's raw state embedding independently against a learned standard 'exploration template' distribution, similar to how brain images are aligned to a reference atlas for consistent cross-scan comparison.
